QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_58

question.text: For the purpose of Regulation 21A, which of the following qualifies as an "associate" of a merchant banker?

answer.text: An entity in which the MB holds a 5% equity stake
A nominee director appointed by a lending institution on the MB's board
An entity that controls 10% or more of the voting rights of the MB directly or indirectly
Any vendor that provides IT services to the MB

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 21A, an "associate" is defined as an entity that controls 10% or more of the voting rights (directly or indirectly), exercises control directly or indirectly, or has a common director (excluding nominee directors and independent directors with recusal). **The Trap:** A nominee director does NOT make entities associates — this is a specific carve-out. A 5% stake is below the 10% threshold. IT vendors have no ownership/control nexus. **Cross-Exam Context:** Associate definitions vary across SEBI regulations — NISM Series IX uses the Regulation 21A definition, while other modules may use different thresholds. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Map out your entity's entire ownership chain to identify all associates — indirect control through layered structures is equally captured by this definition.
